fact: Contact occurred between Car 10 and Car 31 at Turn 3.
infringement: Alleged breach of Appendix L, Chapter IV Art 2(d) of the FIA International Sporting Code.
reason: The Stewards reviewed video and in-car evidence. Car 10 (GAS) moved to overtake Car 31 (OCO) on the inside at Turn 3. A minor collision occurred between the two cars just after the apex. The evidence showed that the front axle of GAS’s car was ahead of the mirror of OCO’s car prior to and at the apex and Car 10 was driven in a fully controlled manner. Applying the Driving Standards Guidelines, GAS was entitled to racing room through Turn 3. However, OCO had already turned-in and was committed to the corner at the point where the collision occurred. The Stewards determined that no driver was wholly or predominantly to blame for the collision and for that reason took no further action.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 10 - Alleged causing a collision with Car 31.pdf

fact: Alleged Forcing another driver off the track at Turn 3
infringement: Alleged breach of Appendix L, Chapter IV, Article 2b) of the FIA International Sporting Code.
reason: The Stewards reviewed video and in-car video evidence. Car 30 (LAW) attempted to overtake Car 11 (PER) on the outside at Turn 3. Wheel to wheel contact occurred between the cars at the apex and LAW went wide and rejoined behind Car 11. LAW’s front axle was not ahead of PER’s front axle at the apex and applying the Driving Standard Guidelines, the Stewards determined that LAW was not entitled to space at the exit of Turn 3 and therefore he was not forced off by PER.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 11 - Alleged forcing another driver off the track.pdf

fact: Alleged pitlane incident – Working on the car in the fast lane
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B1.6.1e of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards reviewed video evidence and determined as follows. During the Qualifying session, which was suspended due to a red flag incident, and while the cars were lined up to leave the pit lane, Car 12 was waiting to blend into the fast lane. Car 12 had inched slightly into the fast lane with a very small portion of the front wing in the fast lane. A team member assisted in pushing the car back a little to allow for that part to not hinder the fast lane. While the car was touched by the team member (to push it back), this did not constitute a breach of Article B1.6.1e which prohibits work being done on the car, which clearly was not the case here. In fact, the team member was taking steps to not hinder the other drivers in the fast lane, which was entirely appropriate in the circumstances. We accordingly took no further action.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 12 - Alleged pitlane incident.pdf

fact: Car 18 failed to set a qualifying time within 107% of the fastest time set in Q1.
infringement: Article B2.4.3b of the FIA F1 Regulations
decision: Permission granted for Car 18 to start the Race.
reason: The Stewards consider the circumstances compelling, including the driver's experience and the team's decision to not participate in Qualifying due to a damaged oil line.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 18 - Failing to set a lap time within 107%.pdf

fact: At the end of the reconnaissance laps Car 27 did not reach the grid under its own power.
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B5.2.4 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards reviewed video evidence and determined that Car 27 did not reach the grid under its own power and could therefore not start the Race from the grid. The team removed Car 27 from the grid and returned it to the garage. It did not start the race. In the circumstances, no infringement was committed.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 27 - Start Procedure Infringement.pdf

fact: Car 3 failed to set a qualifying time within 107% of the fastest time set in Q1.
infringement: Article B2.4.3b of the FIA F1 Regulations
decision: The Stewards grant permission for car 3 to start the Race.
reason: The driver has set satisfactory times in practice at this Competition.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 3 - Failing to set a lap time within 107%.pdf

fact: Contact occurred between Car 31 and Car 10 at Turn 3.
infringement: Alleged breach of Appendix L, Chapter IV Art 2(d) of the FIA International Sporting Code.
reason: The Stewards reviewed video and in-car evidence. Car 10 (GAS) moved to overtake Car 31 (OCO) on the inside at Turn 3. A minor collision occurred between the two cars just after the apex. The evidence showed that the front axle of GAS’s car was ahead of the mirror of OCO’s car prior to and at the apex and Car 10 was driven in a fully controlled manner. Applying the Driving Standards Guidelines, GAS was entitled to racing room through Turn 3. However, OCO had already turned-in and was committed to the corner at the point where the collision occurred. The Stewards determined that no driver was wholly or predominantly to blame for the collision and for that reason took no further action.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 31 - Alleged causing a collision with Car 10.pdf

fact: Car 31 exceeded the VSC delta in several mini-sectors during a VSC deployment
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B1.8.5 and/or B5.12.2.a of the FIA F1 Regulations
reason: The Stewards heard from the driver of Car 31 (Esteban Ocon), the team representative and reviewed positioning/marshalling system data, video, timing, telemetry and team radio and determined that Car 31 did not drive unnecessarily slowly during the VSC period. We accordingly took no further action.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 31 - Allegedly driving unnecessarily slowly.pdf

fact: Driving Unnecessarily Slow on the Main Straight
infringement: Breach of Article B1.8.5 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards were satisfied that there was nothing about COL’s driving which was erratic. Given his mechanical problem, he was not driving 'unnecessarily' slowly. The Driver positioned his Car to ensure that he could reach the designated exit point in the event that the problem with his Car was not resolved before he reached it, the Stewards did not consider that the actions of the Driver were unsafe and therefore determined to take no further action.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 43 - Allegedly Driving Unnecessarily Slow.pdf

fact: Failing to set a qualifying time within 107% of the fastest time set in Q1.
infringement: Article B2.4.3b of the FIA F1 Regulations
decision: The Stewards grant permission for car 55, Carlos Sainz, to start the Race.
reason: The driver has set satisfactory times in practice at this Competition.
source: 2026 Australian Grand Prix - Decision - Car 55 - Failing to set a lap time within 107%.pdf

fact: Car 10 allegedly impeded Car 3 at Turn 14
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B4.1.1 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards heard from the driver of Car 10 (Pierre Gasly), the driver of Car 3 (Max Verstappen) and the team representatives and reviewed telemetry, team radio, video and in-car video evidence. Car 3 was on a push lap and came across Car 10 on what the driver of Car 3 described as his optimal racing line at the exit of turn 14. Car 3 had to drive around Car 10 but did not lift. The driver of Car 10 explained that he was aware of Car 3's approach and considered that the safest option was to stay to the left edge of the track. The driver of Car 3 said that the position of Car 10 affected his preferred line but he said that he was not impeded. Although the Stewards considered that the driver of Car 10 could theoretically have taken a different approach to the turn and stayed to the right at the exit, given the concession by the driver of Car 3 that he was not unnecessarily impeded by Car 10 in this case, we determined to take no further action.
source: 2026 Chinese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 10 - Alleged impeding of Car 3.pdf

fact: Car 11 failed to set a qualifying time within 107% of the fastest time set in SQ1.
infringement: Article B2.2.3b of the FIA F1 Regulations
decision: The Stewards grant permission for car 11 to start the Sprint.
reason: The driver has set satisfactory times in practice at this Competition.
source: 2026 Chinese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 11 - Failing to set a lap time within 107%.pdf

fact: Car 12 Allegedly Impeded Car 1 at Turn 1
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B4.1.1 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards heard from the driver of Car 12 (Kimi Antonelli), the driver of Car 1 (Lando Norris), team representatives and video, telemetry, team radio and in-car video evidence. The driver of Car 1 (Lando Norris) told us that he was on a “pushing warm up lap” and not on a push lap. In other words, he was not actively seeking to set a meaningful lap time when Car 12 (Kimi Antonelli) came out of the pit lane and onto the track. Had Car 1 been on a push lap then, given the position of Car 12 on the track, Car 12 would have unnecessarily impeded Car 1. In the light of the clear position taken by the driver of Car 1 that he was not impeded by Car 12 as he was not seeking to set a meaningful lap time, we took no further action.
source: 2026 Chinese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 12 - Alleged impeding of Car 1.pdf

fact: A collision occurred between Cars 23 and 11 at turn 16.
infringement: Alleged breach of Appendix L, Chapter IV, Article 2 d) of the FIA International Sporting Code.
reason: The Stewards heard from the driver of Car 23 (Alexander Albon), the driver of Car 11 (Sergio Perez), team representatives and reviewed video, team radio and in-car video evidence. The Driver of Car 11 explained that he was on a race-run lap and was about to commence another. He had seen Car 23 some distance behind when he had exited turn 14. Car 23 was on an out lap and gained significantly on Car 11 through turn 15 and on the approach to turn 16. Car 23 moved to overtake Car 11 on the inside at turn 16 and a collision occurred. The Driver of Car 11 said that he did not see Car 23, his virtual mirror was not working and he had not received any warning from his team of Car 23’s approach. This was confirmed by his team radio. The team explained that they had assumed that Car 23 would hold position behind Car 11 and had not noticed the extent to which Car 23 had gained on Car 11 and for that reason did not warn the Driver of Car 11. The Driver of Car 23 said that he believed from the wide line taken by Car 11 at the entry to turn 16 that Car 11 was letting him through. Both Drivers were surprised by the closing speeds. The collision was therefore the result of a misunderstanding contributed to by a lack of communication by the team to the Driver of Car 11. Both Drivers accepted that neither of them was wholly or predominantly to blame for it. The Stewards therefore determined to take no further action.
source: 2026 Japanese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 23 - Alleged incident with car 11.pdf

fact: Car 55 was travelling slowly ahead of Car 30 at the exit of turn 11.
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B1.8.5 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards heard from the driver of Car 30 (Liam Lawson), the driver of Car 55 (Carlos Sainz), team representatives and reviewed team radio and in-car video evidence. Car 55 passed Car 30 after turn 9 and then slowed considerably through turns 10 and 11 with Car 30 close behind. It appeared from the video that the Driver of Car 55 had unnecessarily slowed to baulk Car 30 after Car 55 had been affected by Car 30 at turn 9, gone wide and then abandoned his push lap. However, the Driver of Car 55 explained that he had slowed because he had received a message from his team that another car, Car 34 (Jak Crawford), was approaching from behind on a push lap and the Driver of Car 55 was slowing only to ensure that he did not impede Car 34. The Driver of Car 55’s account was confirmed by the team radio and Car 34 passed both Car 55 and Car 30 after the exit of turn 11. The Stewards therefore determined to take no further action.
source: 2026 Japanese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 30 - Alleged incident with car 55.pdf

fact: Incident between Cars 44 and 3 between turns 14 and 15.
infringement: Alleged breach of Article B1.8.5 of the FIA F1 Regulations.
reason: The Stewards heard from the team representatives and reviewed video and team radio and in-car video evidence. Having reviewed video and team radio, the Stewards find that the Driver of Car 44 (Lewis Hamilton), who had been given no warning from the team of the approach of Car 3 (Max Verstappen), was travelling in the middle of the track between Turn 14 and Turn 15. As Car 3 closed on Car 44, Car 44 made an initial movement suggesting a move to the left, but ultimately remained to the right-hand side of the track. Car 3 approached with a significant speed differential but was able to pass Car 44 without being required to brake, lift, or deviate from its intended racing line. In this case, the Stewards are satisfied that, although Car 3 was momentarily influenced by the positioning of Car 44, it was not forced to take evasive action. The Stewards also took into account that, through his team representative, the Driver of Car 3 said that the situation was not considered dangerous from his perspective. Consistent with the position taken with respect to incidents of this type in free practice sessions where an alleged impeding is not considered dangerous or potentially so, the Stewards determined to take no further action. However, the team is reminded to ensure that timely warnings are given to its drivers of the approach of other cars.
source: 2026 Japanese Grand Prix - Decision - Car 44 - Alleged incident with car 3.pdf
